title: The Best Amazon Prime Day Headphone Deals for Every Kind of Listener
author: Chris Haslam
text: Amazon Prime Day June 2026Absolute Best Amazon Prime Day DealsYeti Cooler DealsOura Ring DealsApple DealsTech DealsFitness DealsDeals Under $100Apple Watch DealsHeadphones DealsNow ReadingVacuum DealsAir Conditioner DealsHydrow DealsAll products featured on WIRED are independently selected by our editors. However, we may receive compensation from retailers and/or from purchases of products through these links. Learn more.looking for new noise-canceling cans for your summer vacation, earbuds for the gym, or luxury designs to take the edge off your commute? These Prime Day headphone deals are for you. Amazon’s annual sales bonanza (which ends tonight) always delivers on audio deals, but to make life easier, we’ve curated the best Prime Day headphone deals with the lowest prices, and only recommend products our experts have tried and tested themselves.Also be sure to browse our roundups of the Absolute Best Prime Day Deals and the Best Prime Day Tech Deals, or dip into our Amazon Prime Day live blog for real-time updates.Updated 6AM ET, June 26: We've added deals on the Sony WH-CH720N and the Belkin SoundForm Mini.Deals on HeadphonesPhotograph: Ryan WaniataSonyWH-1000XM6$460 $378 (18% off) Amazon$458 $378 (17% off) Walmart$458 $378 (17% off) Best BuyIt will be a while before you see Sony’s shiny new $650 1000X The Collexion discounted, but don’t worry, because the on-sale Sony WH-1000XM6 remain one of the finest noise-canceling headphones ever. They’re our favorite headphones for blocking out even the most disruptive background noise, while also offering superb audio performance no matter which genre of music you’re listening to.Photograph: Parker HallPhotograph: Parker HallPhotograph: Parker HallNothingHeadphone (1)$299 $214 (28% off) AmazonMost PopularSmart HomeGoogle’s New Smart Speaker Takes the LeadBy Nena Farrell GearThe $25K Slate Is the Most Affordable EV Truck in the USBy Jeremy WhiteGearThe 99 Absolute Best Prime Day Deals on Our Favorite ProductsBy Louryn StrampeDealsYeti Tundra Coolers Are on Rare Sale TodayBy Martin CizmarA cheaper, poppier follow-up to Nothing’s superb Headphone (1), these stylish noise-canceling headphones look great and boast a full-bodied sound profile that’s perfect for pop and electronic music. There’s a slick app for adjusting sound profiles, battery life can hit 135 hours with ANC off, and there’s even USB-C audio cable included for hi-res listening.Photograph: Adrienne SoPhotograph: Adrienne SoPhotograph: Adrienne SoPhotograph: Adrienne SoAppleAirPods Max 2$530 $399 (25% off) Walmart$549 Amazon$530 AppleWith the same gorgeous, comfortable, iconic design as its predecessor, Apple’s AirPods Max 2 bring improved noise canceling and Bluetooth connectivity. Apple also upgraded software features, like gesture controls and Adaptive Audio. They’re a bit pricey at the RRP, but this discount is decent for Apple fans.Photograph: Ryan WaniataBoseQuietComfort Ultra$449 $269 (40% off) Amazon$449 $379 (16% off) BoseMost PopularSmart HomeGoogle’s New Smart Speaker Takes the LeadBy Nena Farrell GearThe $25K Slate Is the Most Affordable EV Truck in the USBy Jeremy WhiteGearThe 99 Absolute Best Prime Day Deals on Our Favorite ProductsBy Louryn StrampeDealsYeti Tundra Coolers Are on Rare Sale TodayBy Martin CizmarIt’s a testament to the quality of the Bose QuietComfort Ultra that they remain one of the best buys despite being over two years old. They’re supremely comfortable and have impressive spatialized audio, and because they’re Bose, they come with sensationally good, customizable noise cancellation technology. Though they don't sound quite as good, the regular Bose QuietComfort ($179) are also on sale.Courtesy of AmazonSonyWH-CH720N$180 $79 (56% off) Amazon$178 $100 (44% off) Best BuyThey may not be the best headphones, but these Sony cans are very affordable, and they are less than half price right now. There's no case, but the sound quality and noise-canceling are excellent for the money. They are also built to last and offer great battery life.Courtesy of BelkinBelkinSoundform Mini$33 $25 (24% off) Amazon$35 WalmartMost PopularSmart HomeGoogle’s New Smart Speaker Takes the LeadBy Nena Farrell GearThe $25K Slate Is the Most Affordable EV Truck in the USBy Jeremy WhiteGearThe 99 Absolute Best Prime Day Deals on Our Favorite ProductsBy Louryn StrampeDealsYeti Tundra Coolers Are on Rare Sale TodayBy Martin CizmarSturdy, with padded ear cups, and volume limiting to protect your kid's hearing (85 decibels), these are the best kids' headphones. Sound quality is fine for the price, and they boast 30 hours of battery life and are water-resistant.Earbud DealsPhotograph: Parker HallReview: Apple AirPods Pro 3Review: Apple AirPods Pro 3AppleAirPods Pro 3$249 $179 (28% off) Amazon$250 $180 (28% off) Best Buy$249 AppleIf you’re wedded to the Apple ecosystem and live for seamless integration and instant pairing, the AirPods Pro 3 are the best wireless earbuds for you, especially when they’re on sale. The latest generation has up to eight hours of battery, ANC is twice as good, and there’s also heart-rate sensing, workout tracking, and translation.Photograph: Parker HallNothingEar (a)$79 $53 (33% off) Amazon$99 $56 (43% off) NothingMost PopularSmart HomeGoogle’s New Smart Speaker Takes the LeadBy Nena Farrell GearThe $25K Slate Is the Most Affordable EV Truck in the USBy Jeremy WhiteGearThe 99 Absolute Best Prime Day Deals on Our Favorite ProductsBy Louryn StrampeDealsYeti Tundra Coolers Are on Rare Sale TodayBy Martin CizmarBetter looking than the AirPods they’re aping, Nothing’s transparent little buds are a great buy, even at twice the price. The 11 millimeter dynamic drivers boast an open, natural midrange that’s impossible not to enjoy, while ANC delivers quiet better than most budget options. Sound quality won’t worry Sony or Bowers & Wilkins, but they’re a classy design with great fit, intuitive stem controls, app EQ, and fast pairing.Photograph: Ryan WaniataSoundcore by AnkerAeroclip$170 $150 (12% off) SoundcoreBose started the earbud-or-earing trend with the Ultra Open (8/10), but Anker’s headphone division made open-ear listening far more affordable. The AeroClip are light, comfortable, easy to use, and come with extra grips for small ears, and while the sound remains deliberately open for traffic, coworkers and the kids, quality remains clear and enjoyable. Great for sport, or just not feeling plugged in the whole time.Photograph: Julian ChokkattuPhotograph: Parker HallPhotograph: Parker HallGooglePixel Buds Pro 2$229 $161 (30% off) Amazon$229 Best Buy$229 GoogleMost PopularSmart HomeGoogle’s New Smart Speaker Takes the LeadBy Nena Farrell GearThe $25K Slate Is the Most Affordable EV Truck in the USBy Jeremy WhiteGearThe 99 Absolute Best Prime Day Deals on Our Favorite ProductsBy Louryn StrampeDealsYeti Tundra Coolers Are on Rare Sale TodayBy Martin CizmarThe most comfortable non-stem earbuds this reviewer has ever used, with a tiny case as pocketable as AirPods. Google updated the software back in 2025 to add adaptive audio, protection against loud noise, improved Gemini integration and head gesture control that’s not as silly as it sounds. A classy choice with the best integration for Android users.Photograph: Ryan WaniataBoseUltra Open Earbuds$299 $199 (33% off) Amazon$299 $199 (33% off) Bose$299 $199 (33% off) Adorama$299 $199 (33% off) Best BuyThe best-sounding open earbuds offer crystal clear audio and plenty of instrumental detail. The rollable build delivers a secure fit with minimal discomfort, though it can take a few tries to attach them correctly. Battery life is around seven and a half hours, and they have an IPX4 rating, meaning they’re resistant to splashes and light rain. You also get advanced Bose features like multipoint pairing and Bose Spatial Audio.Deals on Sport HeadphonesPhotograph: Ryan WaniataBeatsPowerbeats Pro 2$250 $180 (28% off) Amazon$249 $180 (28% off) Walmart$250 AppleFeaturing the same H2 chip that powers the AirPods Pro 2, the PowerBeats Pro 2 impressed our reviewer, who praised the “crazy comfy” fit and dynamic sound. There’s also ANC and transparency modes for both road safety and gym sanity, wireless charging, IP57 durability, full-on bass-forward audio quality, and built-in heart rate monitoring.Most PopularSmart HomeGoogle’s New Smart Speaker Takes the LeadBy Nena Farrell GearThe $25K Slate Is the Most Affordable EV Truck in the USBy Jeremy WhiteGearThe 99 Absolute Best Prime Day Deals on Our Favorite ProductsBy Louryn StrampeDealsYeti Tundra Coolers Are on Rare Sale TodayBy Martin CizmarCourtesy: AmazonJLab AudioGo Sport+$30 $19 (37% off) AmazonThe JLab Audio Go Air Sport were our favorite budget sports headphone and remain so, but remarkably JLab have updated the design—now called the Go Sport+—improved the specifications and cut the price. You won’t be wowed by audio performance, but the earhooks work well, they have IP55 sweat resistance, 9+ hours of battery, multipoint Bluetooth, and comprehensive app support. Hard to ignore while they’re on sale for $20.Read nextRead nextThe Best Vacuums We've Tried Are Still on Sale—For Now
